question.text: Under the presumptive scheme of taxation (Section 44AD), income from exchange-traded derivatives is assessed at:

answer.text: 6% of the turnover, without allowing deductions for expenses, if total turnover does not exceed Rs. 2 crores
8% of the turnover if total turnover does not exceed Rs. 5 crores
10% of the profit, regardless of turnover
Presumptive taxation is not available for derivatives trading

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 44AD, if total F&O turnover ≤ Rs. 2 crores, the trader can declare 6% of turnover as income without claiming expense deductions. This simplifies tax filing for small traders. **The Trap:** 6% is for DIGITAL transactions under the enhanced limit. Also, turnover exceeding Rs. 2 Cr but ≤ Rs. 10 Cr with >95% digital may not require tax audit. The 8% rate is for non-digital businesses. **Cross-Exam Context:** Presumptive taxation for derivatives is tested in Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** F&O turnover ≤ Rs. 2 Cr: 6% presumptive (Section 44AD). Above Rs. 10 Cr: mandatory tax audit regardless.
